output: Authored §11 (consult-the-doc escape hatch footer with 14 named redesign anchors and three trip-wire signals from :599-604) and completed self-review polish pass. Doc final length 676 lines, 26 TODO(phase-1) markers labeled, no SQL queries against topics WHERE environment='dev' for trip-wire purposes (the three matches are warning text). All 14 self-review check items verified.

task_updates.executor_notes: §11 footer authored. Opens 'Every section that could fork on a Phase-1 deviation defaults to ABORT to §8 rollback and consult docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md.' Cross-references §1 out-of-scope, §3 final two preconditions, §5 Branch-A note, §7 last row. Lists all 14 named redesign anchors: :117-123, :188-191, :212-223, :223, :596, :599-604, :600-601, :611, :627, :633, :642-644, :646-653, :651, :684. Re-lists three trip-wire signals from :599-604: (1) partial-publish ×2 cross-window (Phase-1 telemetry + Phase-2 topics) → ABORT + restore topic_publications; (2) collision-override rate >10% → TUNE per :603/:670/:673; (3) record_observation <1/run sustained → drop tool (post-cutover concern per :602). Closes 'When in doubt, ABORT to §8 and re-read the redesign doc.' Self-review polish pass complete: (1) all pass_to_pass expectations map to sections; (2) §6 trip-wire tally uses prod-only count + Phase-1 telemetry sum — NO query against topics WHERE environment='dev' (verified by grep: the three 'environment=dev' matches are all warning text saying NOT to query this); (3) §5 Branch B names Discord-side count as PRIMARY and SQL marker as nice-to-have secondary; (4) §1 banner lists SIX TODO(phase-1) items (a)-(f) including run-source discriminator and explicit out-of-scope clause for third mechanism; (5) §3 preconditions includes 'Phase 1 shipped Branch A or Branch B (not a third mechanism)' AND 'Run-source discriminator present' as separate gates; (6) §7 decision tree includes third-mechanism row that explicitly covers run-source-discriminator-absent; (7) §9 step 1 names full migration hand-off chain (.migrations_staging/ → supabase/migrations/ → supabase db push → verify in supabase_migrations.schema_migrations); (8) §10 DROP block uses same hand-off chain; (9) §5 verification anchors on next observed live_update_editor_runs row not fixed window; (10) §7 partial-publish threshold is '(Phase-1 telemetry) + (Phase-2 prod) cumulative ≥ 2' with :600-601 citation; (11) §7 lease symptoms split into three distinct rows; (12) §9 cross-checks against :212-223 + src/common/storage_handler.py:652-1968 NOT docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md; (13) SQL is environment='prod' with `-- swap to 'dev'` comments and 'dev has no partials' annotation; (14) length 676 lines (within 200-700 acceptable), schema referenced via anchors not duplicated, _legacy_* deletion date is operator-filled blank, 26 TODO(phase-1) markers visibly labeled across the doc. Anchor references resolve to real lines in source files (verified in T1 audit).

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. §11 footer points back to docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md anchors with all 14 named anchors re-listed: :117-123, :188-191, :212-223, :223, :596, :599-604, :600-601, :611, :627, :633, :642-644, :646-653, :651, :684. Three trip-wire signals from :599-604 re-listed verbatim: (1) partial-publish ×2 cross-window (Phase-1 telemetry + Phase-2 topics) → ABORT + restore topic_publications; (2) collision-override rate >10% → TUNE (citing :603/:670/:673); (3) record_observation <1/run sustained → drop tool (post-cutover concern per :602). Self-review confirms: length 676 lines (within 200-700 acceptable range, ~70% above ~400 target but appropriate given six runbook-required SQL queries + 12-row precondition table + 15-row decision tree + migration body); schema referenced via redesign anchors throughout, not duplicated; six TODO(phase-1) markers a-f visibly labeled with 26 total occurrences across the doc; _legacy_* deletion date is operator-filled blank ('__________' with 'Do not hardcode a date' instruction); tone is terse imperative SQL-first matching docs/runbook-payments.md (Fast Triage section title, decision tables, copy-paste SQL blocks, no narrative prose).
